free_text: j-forbidden (d,p) stripping reactions on C12, O16 and
Mg24
Institute for Chemical Research
The beam was monitored by a 500 u solid state
detector counting the elastically scattered
deutrons from the target
2mm thick silicon solid state detector of
surface barrier type in conjunction with a
1024 channel PHA was used to detect the proton
groups from the (d,p) reaction.
A broad range magnetic spectrograph with
nuclear plates of Sakura NRE-1 of 100 u thick
was used for higher order resolution was
necessary to resolve the individual levels.
The plates were covered with Al foils of
relevant to avoid particles other than proton.

V.D
ANG-CM is polar angle (c.m.) between beam and proton
Beam intensity: The uncertainty in the beam current
measurement depended on the beam intensity and
estimated to about 20%
[EN-RSL] overall energy resolution of the system was
about 0.9%
- Target is natural.
- Chemical-form of target: Carbon foil prepared from
natural methane gas.
- Physical-form of target is gas.
- Target-thickness is 0.87 mg/cm2.
- Target is self supported. self-supported carbon
foils prepared from natural methane gas
Statistical uncertainty
1. PWBA (Plane wave Born approximation)
2. DWBA (Distorted wave Born approximation)
Plotted in Fig. 4 of J. Phys. Soc. Jpn 25, 1
